title: Contributing to brapiR2
text: Thank interest contributing brapiR2!

title: Contributing to brapiR2
text: Fork repository GitHub Clone fork locally Create branch feature fix Make changes following style guide Test changes devtools::check() Submit pull request

title: Contributing to brapiR2
text: Follow tidyverse style guide Use roxygen2 documentation exported functions must @examples Use cli user-facing messages (message() cat())

title: Contributing to brapiR2
text: Add function appropriate module file (R/core.R, R/germplasm.R, etc.) Follow existing pattern: con first argument, ... query params Use brapi_get() brapi_post_search() internally Add function NAMESPACE exports Write tests tests/testthat/ Update NEWS.md

title: Contributing to brapiR2
text: Use GitHub issue tracker Include minimal reproducible example Note BrAPI server ’re connecting (relevant)

title: Contributing to brapiR2
text: Please respectful constructive. follow Contributor Covenant code conduct.

title: Genomic Selection Pipeline with brapiR2
text: vignette demonstrates complete genomic selection (GS) workflow using brapiR2 pull phenotypic genotypic data directly BrAPI-compliant server R, running GS models rrBLUP. brapi_get_dosage_matrix() function returns standard numeric matrix (samples × markers) directly compatible major GS packages:

what: Step 1: Connect and Fetch Phenotypic Data
title: Genomic Selection Pipeline with brapiR2
code: library(brapiR2) library(dplyr)  con <- brapi_connection("https://my-breedbase.org") con <- brapi_login(con, "username", "password")  # Optional: enable caching so re-runs don't hit the server again con <- brapi_cache_enable(con, ttl = 7200)  # Get phenotype data in wide format (one row per plot, one column per trait) pheno_raw <- brapi_study_data(con, "my_study_id") pheno_raw #> # A tibble: 250 x 8 #>   observationUnitDbId germplasmDbId germplasmName  studyDbId #>   <chr>               <chr>         <chr>          <chr> #> 1 ou001               g001          Variety_001    study_01 #> 2 ou002               g002          Variety_002    study_01 #> # ... with 246 more rows, and 4 more variables: #> #   Grain_Yield_t_ha <chr>, Days_to_Heading <chr>, #> #   Plant_Height_cm <chr>, Thousand_Grain_Weight <chr>  # Convert trait columns to numeric and drop missing pheno <- pheno_raw |>   mutate(across(Grain_Yield_t_ha:Thousand_Grain_Weight, as.numeric)) |>   filter(!is.na(Grain_Yield_t_ha))  nrow(pheno) #> [1] 241

title: Genomic Selection Pipeline with brapiR2
code: # List available variant sets to find the right genotyping dataset vsets <- brapi_variant_sets(con) vsets #> # A tibble: 3 x 8 #>   variantSetDbId variantSetName callSetCount variantCount #>   <chr>          <chr>                 <int>        <int> #> 1 vs001          SNP50K_2022              250        45230 #> 2 vs002          GBS_v2_2022              250        82100 #> 3 vs003          DArTseq_panel            250        14500  # Get dosage matrix: rows = samples (callSetDbIds), cols = markers (variantDbIds) # Values: 0 (hom ref), 1 (het), 2 (hom alt), NA (missing) dosage <- brapi_get_dosage_matrix(con, "vs001") #> i Fetching allele matrix for variant set 'vs001'... #> i Encoding 11307500 genotype calls as allele dosages... #> v Dosage matrix ready: 250 samples x 45230 markers.  dim(dosage) #> [1]   250 45230  # Preview dosage values dosage[1:4, 1:6] #>             SNP_chr1_1234 SNP_chr1_5678 SNP_chr2_234 SNP_chr2_890 ... #> Variety_001             0             1            2            0 #> Variety_002             2             0            0            1 #> Variety_003             1             1            1            1 #> Variety_004             0             0            2            0  # Get marker map for downstream QTL / Manhattan plots markers <- brapi_get_marker_map(con, "vs001") markers #> # A tibble: 45230 x 4 #>    variantDbId    variantName   referenceName  start #>    <chr>          <chr>         <chr>          <int> #>  1 var001         SNP_chr1_1234 1A              1234 #>  2 var002         SNP_chr1_5678 1A              5678 #>  # ... with 45228 more rows

title: Genomic Selection Pipeline with brapiR2
code: # Match on germplasmName (rownames of dosage = callSetDbId = germplasmName # on most servers; adjust if your server uses a different identifier) common <- intersect(rownames(dosage), pheno$germplasmName) length(common) #> [1] 237  pheno_aligned <- pheno |>   filter(germplasmName %in% common) |>   arrange(match(germplasmName, common))  geno_aligned <- dosage[common, ]  # Quick missing-data summary mean(is.na(geno_aligned)) #> [1] 0.0312     # ~3.1% missing calls — impute before modelling

title: Genomic Selection Pipeline with brapiR2
code: # Simple mean imputation (column means) col_means <- colMeans(geno_aligned, na.rm = TRUE) for (j in seq_len(ncol(geno_aligned))) {   geno_aligned[is.na(geno_aligned[, j]), j] <- col_means[j] }  # Or use rrBLUP's A.mat which handles missing data internally

what: Step 5: Run Genomic Selection with rrBLUP
title: Genomic Selection Pipeline with brapiR2
code: library(rrBLUP)  # Mixed model: phenotype ~ overall mean + random marker effects result <- mixed.solve(   y = pheno_aligned$Grain_Yield_t_ha,   Z = geno_aligned )  # Estimated marker effects length(result$u) #> [1] 45230  # GEBVs (Genomic Estimated Breeding Values) gebvs <- geno_aligned %*% result$u + result$beta head(gebvs) #>               [,1] #> Variety_001  4.82 #> Variety_002  5.14 #> Variety_003  4.67 #> Variety_004  5.31 #> Variety_005  4.91 #> Variety_006  5.09  hist(gebvs,   main = "Distribution of GEBVs — Grain Yield",   xlab = "GEBV (t/ha)", col = "steelblue" )

title: Genomic Selection Pipeline with brapiR2
code: # ---- BGLR (Bayesian regression) ---- library(BGLR)  fm_bglr <- BGLR(   y = pheno_aligned$Grain_Yield_t_ha,   ETA = list(MRK = list(X = geno_aligned, model = "BRR")),   nIter = 6000, burnIn = 1000,   verbose = FALSE ) gebvs_bglr <- geno_aligned %*% fm_bglr$ETA$MRK$b  # ---- sommer (mixed model with G matrix) ---- library(sommer) library(AGHmatrix)  # Build genomic relationship matrix G <- Gmatrix(geno_aligned, method = "VanRaden") #> Gmatrix computation: VanRaden method #> Markers: 45230  Individuals: 237  fm_sommer <- mmer(   Grain_Yield_t_ha ~ 1,   random  = ~ vsr(germplasmName, Gu = G),   rcov    = ~units,   data    = pheno_aligned ) gebvs_sommer <- randef(fm_sommer)$`u:germplasmName`

title: Genomic Selection Pipeline with brapiR2
code: set.seed(42) n <- nrow(pheno_aligned) folds <- sample(rep(1:5, length.out = n)) cors <- numeric(5)  for (k in 1:5) {   train <- folds != k   test <- folds == k    fit <- mixed.solve(     y = pheno_aligned$Grain_Yield_t_ha[train],     Z = geno_aligned[train, ]   )    pred <- geno_aligned[test, ] %*% fit$u + fit$beta   cors[k] <- cor(as.numeric(pred), pheno_aligned$Grain_Yield_t_ha[test]) }  cat(sprintf(   "5-fold CV prediction accuracy: %.3f ± %.3f\n",   mean(cors), sd(cors) )) #> 5-fold CV prediction accuracy: 0.612 ± 0.054

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
text: brapiR2 provides pipe-friendly, stateless access BrAPI v2 servers. Unlike stateful clients navigate setting “current” program trial, brapiR2 functions independent — pass connection object get tibble back. makes ideal scripting, pipelines, building downstream tools.

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
text: Every function takes con first argument. global state modified.
code: library(brapiR2)  # The BrAPI test server is public and requires no authentication con <- brapi_connection("https://test-server.brapi.org") con #> -- BrAPI Connection -- #> Server : https://test-server.brapi.org #> Version: v2 #> Token  : <none> #> Cache  : disabled

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
code: library(dplyr)  # List all breeding programs programs <- brapi_programs(con) programs #> # A tibble: 3 x 10 #>   programDbId programName    abbreviation commonCropName #>   <chr>       <chr>          <chr>        <chr> #> 1 program1    Wheat Breeding WB           Wheat #> 2 program2    Maize Program  MP           Maize #> 3 program3    Rice Research  RR           Rice #> # ... with 6 more variables: programType <chr>, leadPersonDbId <chr>, #> #   leadPersonName <chr>, documentationURL <list>, externalReferences <list>, #> #   additionalInfo <list>  # List trials in the first program trials <- brapi_trials(con, programDbId = programs$programDbId[1]) trials #> # A tibble: 3 x 12 #>   trialDbId trialName        programDbId programName    active #>   <chr>     <chr>            <chr>       <chr>          <lgl> #> 1 trial1    Yield Trial 2022 program1    Wheat Breeding TRUE #> 2 trial2    Drought Study    program1    Wheat Breeding TRUE #> 3 trial3    Regional Test    program1    Wheat Breeding FALSE  # List studies within a trial studies <- brapi_studies(con, trialDbId = trials$trialDbId[1]) studies #> # A tibble: 3 x 15 #>   studyDbId studyName       trialDbId trialName        locationDbId #>   <chr>     <chr>           <chr>     <chr>            <chr> #> 1 study1    Ithaca 2022     trial1    Yield Trial 2022 location1 #> 2 study2    Geneva 2022     trial1    Yield Trial 2022 location2 #> 3 study3    Riverhead 2022  trial1    Yield Trial 2022 location3

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
code: # Get analysis-ready wide format: one row per plot, one column per trait data <- brapi_study_data(con, "study1") data #> # A tibble: 2 x 6 #>   observationUnitDbId observationUnitName germplasmDbId germplasmName #>   <chr>               <chr>               <chr>         <chr> #> 1 ou1                 Plot 1              germ1         Variety_A #> 2 ou2                 Plot 2              germ2         Variety_B #> # ... with 2 more variables: `Corn Stalk Height` <chr>, #> #                             `Plant Height` <chr>  # All traits become columns — ready for analysis data |>   mutate(across(c(`Corn Stalk Height`, `Plant Height`), as.numeric)) |>   summarise(across(where(is.numeric), mean, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
code: # List available variant sets (genotyping datasets) vsets <- brapi_variant_sets(con) vsets #> # A tibble: 2 x 8 #>   variantSetDbId variantSetName callSetCount variantCount #>   <chr>          <chr>                 <int>        <int> #> 1 variantSet1    SNP50K                   13           20 #> 2 variantSet2    GBS_v1                   50         1500  # Get marker chromosome / position map markers <- brapi_get_marker_map(con, "variantSet1") markers #> # A tibble: 20 x 4 #>    variantDbId variantName   referenceName  start #>    <chr>       <chr>         <chr>          <int> #>  1 marker1     SNP_001       1A                NA #>  2 marker2     SNP_002       1A                NA #>  # ...  # Get dosage matrix for genomic selection (samples x markers, values 0/1/2) dosage <- brapi_get_dosage_matrix(con, "variantSet1") dim(dosage) #> [1] 13 20        # 13 samples, 20 markers dosage[1:3, 1:5] #>            marker1 marker2 marker3 marker4 marker5 #> callset1         0       1       2       0       1 #> callset2         2       0       1       2       0 #> callset3         1       1       0       1       2

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
text: production BrAPI servers require authentication:
code: # Username/password (BreedBase, BMS, Germinate) con <- brapi_connection("https://my-breedbase.org") con <- brapi_login(con, "username", "password")  # OAuth 2.0 (EBS and similar) con <- brapi_login_oauth2(   con,   client_id     = "my_id",   client_secret = "my_secret",   authorize_url = "https://auth.example.org/authorize",   access_url    = "https://auth.example.org/token" )  # Bearer token (GIGWA, custom servers) con <- brapi_set_token(con, Sys.getenv("BRAPI_TOKEN"))

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
code: # Enable caching — repeated calls within the TTL return instantly con <- brapi_cache_enable(con, ttl = 3600) #> v Caching enabled at ~/.cache/brapiR2 (TTL: 3600s)  # First call: hits the server programs <- brapi_programs(con)  # Second call: reads from disk (sub-millisecond) programs <- brapi_programs(con) #> i Using cached response for /programs.  # Clear all cached files brapi_cache_clear(con) #> v Cleared 5 cached response(s).  # Fetch study data from multiple environments in parallel study_ids <- c("study1", "study2", "study3") all_data <- brapi_fetch_parallel(con, brapi_study_data, study_ids, .workers = 3) #> i Fetching 3 items across 3 workers... #> v Fetched 6 rows from 3 sources. all_data #> # A tibble: 6 x 7 #>   observationUnitDbId studyDbId studyName  germplasmName  `Corn Stalk Height` #>   <chr>               <chr>     <chr>      <chr>          <chr> #> 1 ou1                 study1    Ithaca     Variety_A      180 #> 2 ou2                 study1    Ithaca     Variety_B      195 #> # ...

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
text: QBMS well-established R package BrAPI access widely adopted breeding community. brapiR2 QBMS complementary tools designed different workflows.

title: Getting Started with brapiR2
text: QBMS — navigational, stateful: brapiR2 — functional, pipe-friendly: Choose QBMS interactive exploration console. Choose brapiR2 reproducible scripts, pipelines, need genotypic data caching. Many users use .
code: library(QBMS)  set_crop("Wheat") login_bms("https://my-bms.org", "user", "pass")  list_programs() set_program("Wheat Breeding")  list_trials() set_trial("Yield Trial 2022")  list_studies() set_study("Ithaca 2022")  data <- get_study_data() library(brapiR2) library(dplyr)  con <- brapi_connection("https://my-bms.org") |>   brapi_login("user", "pass")  data <- brapi_programs(con) |>   filter(programName == "Wheat Breeding") |>   pull(programDbId) |>   (\(pid) brapi_trials(con, programDbId = pid))() |>   filter(trialName == "Yield Trial 2022") |>   pull(trialDbId) |>   (\(tid) brapi_studies(con, trialDbId = tid))() |>   filter(studyName == "Ithaca 2022") |>   pull(studyDbId) |>   brapi_study_data(con = con)

title: Multi-Environment Trial (MET) Analysis with brapiR2
text: Multi-environment trial (MET) analysis core workflow plant breeding program: gather phenotypic observations many locations years, decompose genotype × environment (GxE) interaction identify stable, high-performing varieties. vignette shows : Pull phenotypic data multiple BrAPI studies parallel Attach environment metadata (location, season) Run AMMI / GGE biplots metan package Run mixed-model stability analysis lme4

title: Multi-Environment Trial (MET) Analysis with brapiR2
code: library(brapiR2) library(dplyr)  con <- brapi_connection("https://my-breedbase.org") con <- brapi_login(con, "username", "password")  # Cache responses to speed up re-runs and protect server bandwidth con <- brapi_cache_enable(con, ttl = 7200) #> v Caching enabled at ~/.cache/brapiR2 (TTL: 7200s) # Discover all studies in the target trial trials <- brapi_trials(con, programDbId = "wheat_program_01") trials #> # A tibble: 4 x 12 #>   trialDbId   trialName           programDbId active #>   <chr>       <chr>               <chr>       <lgl> #> 1 trial_2022  National Yield 2022 wheat_prog  TRUE #> 2 trial_2021  National Yield 2021 wheat_prog  FALSE #> 3 trial_2020  National Yield 2020 wheat_prog  FALSE #> 4 trial_drght Drought Nursery     wheat_prog  TRUE  # Focus on the current MET studies <- brapi_studies(con, trialDbId = "trial_2022") studies #> # A tibble: 8 x 15 #>   studyDbId  studyName          locationDbId locationName  seasonDbId #>   <chr>      <chr>              <chr>        <chr>         <chr> #> 1 study_e1   Ithaca NY 2022     loc_ithaca   Ithaca        season_2022 #> 2 study_e2   Geneva NY 2022     loc_geneva   Geneva        season_2022 #> 3 study_e3   Riverhead NY 2022  loc_river    Riverhead     season_2022 #> 4 study_e4   State College 2022 loc_psu      State College season_2022 #> 5 study_e5   Wooster OH 2022    loc_wooster  Wooster       season_2022 #> 6 study_e6   Lexington KY 2022  loc_lexing   Lexington     season_2022 #> 7 study_e7   Raleigh NC 2022    loc_raleigh  Raleigh       season_2022 #> 8 study_e8   Gainesville FL 22  loc_gnsvlle  Gainesville   season_2022  study_ids <- studies$studyDbId

what: Step 2: Fetch All Study Data in Parallel
title: Multi-Environment Trial (MET) Analysis with brapiR2
text: brapi_fetch_parallel() uses furrr package run one worker per study concurrently. 8 environments 4 workers, fetch completes roughly time 2 sequential requests.
code: library(future) # plan() sets the parallel backend  # Adjust .workers to match your CPU count or server rate limits met_raw <- brapi_fetch_parallel(   con, brapi_study_data, study_ids,   .workers = 4L ) #> i Fetching 8 items across 4 workers... #> v Fetched 2400 rows from 8 sources.  glimpse(met_raw) #> Rows: 2,400 #> Columns: 10 #> $ observationUnitDbId  <chr> "ou_e1_001", "ou_e1_002", ... #> $ observationUnitName  <chr> "Plot 101", "Plot 102", ... #> $ germplasmDbId        <chr> "g001", "g002", ... #> $ germplasmName        <chr> "Variety_001", "Variety_002", ... #> $ studyDbId            <chr> "study_e1", "study_e1", ... #> $ studyName            <chr> "Ithaca NY 2022", ... #> $ Grain_Yield_t_ha     <chr> "5.12", "4.89", ... #> $ Days_to_Heading      <chr> "65", "68", ... #> $ Plant_Height_cm      <chr> "92", "88", ... #> $ Thousand_Grain_Wt    <chr> "38.4", "41.2", ...

title: Multi-Environment Trial (MET) Analysis with brapiR2
text: study data already carries studyDbId studyName. Join location season data studies tibble richer environment labels.
code: # Bring in location and season columns from the studies tibble env_meta <- studies |>   select(studyDbId, studyName, locationName, seasonDbId)  met <- met_raw |>   left_join(env_meta, by = "studyDbId") |>   mutate(     Grain_Yield_t_ha = as.numeric(Grain_Yield_t_ha),     Days_to_Heading = as.numeric(Days_to_Heading),     Plant_Height_cm = as.numeric(Plant_Height_cm),     Thousand_Grain_Wt = as.numeric(Thousand_Grain_Wt),     env = locationName # short environment label   ) |>   filter(!is.na(Grain_Yield_t_ha))  nrow(met) #> [1] 2311   # some missing values removed  # Confirm we have data from all 8 environments table(met$env) #>   Gainesville       Geneva  Ithaca  Lexington  Raleigh  Riverhead #>           285          289     291         278      283        286 #>  State College  Wooster #>            304      295

what: Step 4: Means Matrix for GxE Analysis
title: Multi-Environment Trial (MET) Analysis with brapiR2
text: Many GxE methods (AMMI, GGE biplot) require two-way table genotype means per environment.
code: library(tidyr)  # Compute adjusted means (plot-level mean per genotype × environment) means <- met |>   group_by(germplasmName, env) |>   summarise(yield = mean(Grain_Yield_t_ha, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")  # Wide format: rows = genotypes, columns = environments means_wide <- means |>   pivot_wider(names_from = env, values_from = yield)  means_wide #> # A tibble: 300 x 9 #>   germplasmName  Gainesville Geneva Ithaca Lexington Raleigh Riverhead ... #>   <chr>                <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 Variety_001           5.23   4.98   5.12      5.41    4.87      5.03 #> 2 Variety_002           4.89   5.34   4.91      4.72    5.12      5.28 #> # ...

what: Step 5: AMMI and GGE Biplot with metan
title: Multi-Environment Trial (MET) Analysis with brapiR2
code: library(metan)  # AMMI model: decompose GxE with additive main effects + multiplicative interaction ammi_model <- performs_ammi(   .data = met,   env = env,   gen = germplasmName,   rep = observationUnitDbId, # plot as replicate; adjust if you have block IDs   resp = Grain_Yield_t_ha,   verbose = FALSE )  # AMMI stability value (ASV) — lower = more stable across environments ammi_stable <- ammi_indexes(ammi_model) ammi_stable$Grain_Yield_t_ha |>   arrange(ASV) |>   head(10) #> # A tibble: 10 x 6 #>   GEN          Y     IPCA1   IPCA2    ASV  WAAS #>   <chr>    <dbl>     <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Var_089  5.34    0.00312  0.0041  0.012  0.51 #> 2 Var_214  5.21    0.00891  0.0023  0.035  0.48 #> # ...  # AMMI1 biplot: IPCA1 scores vs mean yield plot(ammi_model, type = "AMMI1") # GGE biplot gge_model <- gge(   .data = met,   env   = env,   gen   = germplasmName,   rep   = observationUnitDbId,   resp  = Grain_Yield_t_ha )  plot(gge_model, type = "which_won_where") #> (biplot showing mega-environments and winning genotypes per environment)

what: Step 6: Mixed Model Stability with lme4
title: Multi-Environment Trial (MET) Analysis with brapiR2
text: unbalanced data need BLUPs rather means, fit mixed model genotype × environment interaction random effect.
code: library(lme4)  # Basic mixed model: GxE interaction as random fm <- lmer(   Grain_Yield_t_ha ~ env + (1 | germplasmName) + (1 | germplasmName:env),   data = met ) summary(fm) #> Random effects: #>  Groups               Name        Variance Std.Dev. #>  germplasmName:env    (Intercept) 0.0812   0.285 #>  germplasmName        (Intercept) 0.1947   0.441 #>  Residual                         0.0534   0.231  # Extract BLUPs for genotypic effects blups <- ranef(fm)$germplasmName blups <- tibble(   germplasmName = rownames(blups),   BLUP          = blups[["(Intercept)"]] ) |>   arrange(desc(BLUP))  head(blups, 10) #> # A tibble: 10 x 2 #>   germplasmName   BLUP #>   <chr>          <dbl> #> 1 Variety_089    0.412 #> 2 Variety_214    0.389 #> 3 Variety_031    0.371 #> # ...  # Variance component partitioning VarCorr(fm) #>  Groups            Name        Std.Dev. #>  germplasmName:env (Intercept)  0.285    # GxE component #>  germplasmName     (Intercept)  0.441    # G (genetic signal) #>  Residual                       0.231  # H^2 broad-sense heritability (across-environment) var_g <- as.numeric(VarCorr(fm)$germplasmName) var_ge <- as.numeric(VarCorr(fm)$`germplasmName:env`) var_e <- sigma(fm)^2 n_env <- length(unique(met$env)) n_rep <- 1 # one plot per genotype per env (adjust if replicated)  H2 <- var_g / (var_g + var_ge / n_env + var_e / (n_env * n_rep)) cat(sprintf("Broad-sense H^2 (across environments): %.3f\n", H2)) #> Broad-sense H^2 (across environments): 0.731

title: Multi-Environment Trial (MET) Analysis with brapiR2
code: # Combine BLUP ranking with stability selection <- blups |>   left_join(     ammi_stable$Grain_Yield_t_ha |>       select(GEN, mean_yield = Y, ASV) |>       rename(germplasmName = GEN),     by = "germplasmName"   ) |>   mutate(     # Normalise both metrics (0 = worst, 1 = best)     rank_yield = rank(BLUP) / n(),     rank_stab  = 1 - rank(ASV) / n(), # lower ASV = more stable = better     score      = 0.6 * rank_yield + 0.4 * rank_stab   ) |>   arrange(desc(score))  # Top 20 selections: high yield + high stability top20 <- head(selection, 20) top20 #> # A tibble: 20 x 6 #>   germplasmName    BLUP mean_yield   ASV rank_yield rank_stab score #>   <chr>           <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Variety_089     0.412       5.34 0.012      0.998     0.997 0.998 #> 2 Variety_214     0.389       5.21 0.035      0.991     0.989 0.991 #> # ...

title: Authors and Citation
text: Joash Joshua Ayo. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ayo J (2026). brapiR2: Tidyverse-Native Client BrAPI v2. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/josh45-source/brapiR2.
code: @Manual{,   title = {brapiR2: A Tidyverse-Native Client for BrAPI v2},   author = {Joash Joshua Ayo},   year = {2026},   url = {https://github.com/josh45-source/brapiR2},   note = {R package version 0.1.0}, }

what: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
text: brapiR2 tidyverse-native, stateless R client BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) specification. provides pipe-friendly access four BrAPI modules — Core, Germplasm, Phenotyping, Genotyping — returning tidy tibbles ready analysis. Developed Joash Joshua Ayo (joashjoshua789@gmail.com).

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
text: brapiR2 complements QBMS — use QBMS interactive exploration, use brapiR2 programmatic pipelines custom tooling.

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
text: Install development version GitHub:

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
code: library(brapiR2) library(dplyr)  # 1. Connect (no global state!) con <- brapi_connection("https://test-server.brapi.org")  # 2. Explore programs and trials brapi_programs(con)  brapi_trials(con) |>   filter(active == TRUE) |>   head()  # 3. Get phenotypic data in analysis-ready wide format data <- brapi_study_data(con, "study_01")  # 4. Get genotypic data as a dosage matrix for GS dosage <- brapi_get_dosage_matrix(con, "variantset_01")  # 5. Parallel fetch across multiple studies all_data <- brapi_fetch_parallel(   con,   brapi_study_data,   ids = c("study_01", "study_02", "study_03"),   .workers = 4 )

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
text: Every function takes connection object first argument returns tibble, making natural chain dplyr:
code: con <- brapi_connection("https://my-breedbase.org", token = "my_token")  # Find all germplasm used in a specific study brapi_observation_units(con, studyDbId = "study_42") |>   select(germplasmDbId, germplasmName) |>   distinct()  # Get marker positions for a genotyping dataset brapi_get_marker_map(con, "my_variantset") |>   filter(referenceName == "chr1") |>   arrange(start)

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
text: Core: Programs, Trials, Studies, Locations, Seasons, Lists, People Germplasm: Germplasm, Pedigrees, Progeny, Attributes, Crosses, Seed Lots Phenotyping: Observation Units, Observations, Variables, Traits, Scales, Methods, Images, Events Genotyping: Samples, Variants, Variant Sets, Calls, Call Sets, References, Allele Matrix

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
code: # Token-based (most BrAPI servers) con <- brapi_connection("https://my-breedbase.org") con <- brapi_login(con, "username", "password")  # OAuth 2.0 (EBS and similar) con <- brapi_login_oauth2(   con,   client_id = "my_id",   client_secret = "my_secret",   authorize_url = "https://auth.example.org/authorize",   access_url = "https://auth.example.org/token" )  # Or set an existing token directly con <- brapi_set_token(con, "my_existing_token")

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
text: QBMS — High-level, stateful BrAPI client interactive use rrBLUP — Genomic selection (use brapiR2 fetch dosage matrix) sommer — Mixed models multi-environment trials

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
text: Contributions welcome! Please see CONTRIBUTING.md guidelines.

title: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification
text: MIT © Joash Joshua Ayo

what: brapiR2: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification — brapiR2-package
title: brapiR2: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification — brapiR2-package
text: Provides pipe-friendly, stateless access every endpoint Breeding API (BrAPI) v2.1 specification. Covers four BrAPI modules — Core, Germplasm, Phenotyping, Genotyping — returning tidy tibbles ready analysis. Features include automatic pagination, async search handling, response caching, parallel batch fetching, convenience functions genomic selection workflows (e.g. dosage matrix extraction). Designed plant breeders bioinformaticians need programmatic access BreedBase, BMS, EBS, GIGWA, Germinate, BrAPI-compliant server.

title: brapiR2: A Tidyverse-Native Client for the BrAPI v2 (Breeding API) Specification — brapiR2-package
text: Maintainer: Joash Joshua Ayo joashjoshua789@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: Get Allele Matrix — brapi_allele_matrix
text: Retrieves genotype calls /allelematrix endpoint returns tidy tibble one row per (variant, callSet) combination. /allelematrix response unique structure (2-D pagination, result$data envelope) use generic brapi_get().

title: Get Allele Matrix — brapi_allele_matrix
text: con brapi_connection() object. variantSetDbId Character NULL. Filter variant set. ... Additional query parameters (e.g. expandHomozygotes, unknownString, sepPhased, sepUnphased).

title: Get Allele Matrix — brapi_allele_matrix
text: tibble columns variantDbId, callSetDbId, genotype.

title: Clear the Response Cache — brapi_cache_clear
text: Removes cached responses cache directory.

title: Clear the Response Cache — brapi_cache_clear
text: con brapi_connection() object caching enabled.

title: Clear the Response Cache — brapi_cache_clear
text: Invisibly returns con.

title: Enable Response Caching — brapi_cache_enable
text: Returns new connection object caching enabled. Cached responses stored JSON files specified directory, keyed URL query parameters.

title: Enable Response Caching — brapi_cache_enable
text: con brapi_connection() object. dir Character. Directory store cached responses. Defaults user cache directory via rappdirs::user_cache_dir(). ttl Numeric. Time--live cached entries seconds. Default 3600 (1 hour).

title: Enable Response Caching — brapi_cache_enable
text: new brapi_con object caching configured.

what: Create a BrAPI Connection Object — brapi_connection
title: Create a BrAPI Connection Object — brapi_connection
text: Creates connection object holds server URL, authentication token, configuration. object passed first argument brapiR2 functions. global state used.

title: Create a BrAPI Connection Object — brapi_connection
code: brapi_connection(   url,   token = NULL,   version = "v2",   page_size = 1000L,   timeout = 120 )

title: Create a BrAPI Connection Object — brapi_connection
text: url Character. Base URL BrAPI server (e.g. "https://test-server.brapi.org"). Trailing slashes removed. token Character NULL. existing Bearer token authentication. NULL, can authenticate later brapi_login() brapi_login_oauth2(). version Character. BrAPI version path segment. Default "v2". page_size Integer. Number records per page paginated requests. Default 1000. timeout Numeric. Request timeout seconds. Default 120.

title: Create a BrAPI Connection Object — brapi_connection
text: S3 object class "brapi_con" (named list).

title: Create a BrAPI Connection Object — brapi_connection
code: # Connect to the public BrAPI test server (no auth needed) con <- brapi_connection("https://test-server.brapi.org") con #>  #> ── BrAPI Connection  #> • Server: <https://test-server.brapi.org> #> • Version: v2 #> • Auth: ✗ no token #> • Page size: 1000 #> • Timeout: 120s #> • Cache: disabled  # Connect with an existing token con <- brapi_connection("https://my-breedbase.org", token = "my_token_here")

title: Parallel Batch Fetching — brapi_fetch_parallel

title: Parallel Batch Fetching — brapi_fetch_parallel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- brapi_connection("https://test-server.brapi.org") study_ids <- c("study_01", "study_02", "study_03") all_data <- brapi_fetch_parallel(con, brapi_study_data, study_ids) } # }

what: Internal: GET a BrAPI Endpoint with Automatic Pagination — brapi_get
title: Internal: GET a BrAPI Endpoint with Automatic Pagination — brapi_get
text: Sends GET request BrAPI endpoint handles pagination transparently. Returns pages concatenated single tibble. caching enabled con (via brapi_cache_enable()), full multi-page result stored JSON file keyed URL + sorted query parameters. Subsequent calls within TTL window skip HTTP request return cached result.

title: Internal: GET a BrAPI Endpoint with Automatic Pagination — brapi_get
Internal: GET a BrAPI Endpoint with Automatic Pagination — brapi_get
text: con brapi_con object. endpoint Character. API endpoint (e.g. "/programs"). query Named list. Query parameters append URL.

title: Internal: GET a BrAPI Endpoint with Automatic Pagination — brapi_get
text: tibble results, empty tibble data.

what: Get Dosage Matrix for Genomic Selection — brapi_get_dosage_matrix
title: Get Dosage Matrix for Genomic Selection — brapi_get_dosage_matrix
text: Fetches genotype data BrAPI server converts numeric dosage matrix (samples × markers) compatible genomic selection packages like rrBLUP, BGLR, sommer.

title: Get Dosage Matrix for Genomic Selection — brapi_get_dosage_matrix
Get Dosage Matrix for Genomic Selection — brapi_get_dosage_matrix
text: con brapi_connection() object. variantSetDbId Character. variant set retrieve. sep Character. Unphased allele separator. Default "/" (e.g. "0/1"). Phased calls using "|" also handled automatically. unknown_string Character. String representing missing data. Default ".".

title: Get Dosage Matrix for Genomic Selection — brapi_get_dosage_matrix
text: numeric matrix: rows = samples (callSetDbIds), columns = markers (variantDbIds). Values integer dosages (0, 1, 2 diploids; 0–N polyploids). Missing calls NA.

title: Get Dosage Matrix for Genomic Selection — brapi_get_dosage_matrix
text: Allele dosage computed splitting genotype string sep ("|" phased calls) counting many alleles non-reference (.e. "0"). Missing calls (unknown_string, ".", "") become NA.

title: Get Dosage Matrix for Genomic Selection — brapi_get_dosage_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- brapi_connection("https://test-server.brapi.org") dosage <- brapi_get_dosage_matrix(con, "variantset_01") dim(dosage) # Use with rrBLUP: # library(rrBLUP) # result <- mixed.solve(y = pheno$yield, Z = dosage) } # }

what: Internal: POST Search to a BrAPI Endpoint — brapi_post_search
title: Internal: POST Search to a BrAPI Endpoint — brapi_post_search
text: Handles BrAPI search pattern: POST /search/{entity} JSON body 200: results response body directly 202: server returns searchResultsDbId; poll GET /search/{entity}/{searchResultsDbId} results ready

title: Internal: POST Search to a BrAPI Endpoint — brapi_post_search
code: brapi_post_search(   con,   endpoint,   body = list(),   poll_interval = 2,   max_polls = 30L )

title: Internal: POST Search to a BrAPI Endpoint — brapi_post_search
text: con brapi_con object. endpoint Character. search endpoint (e.g. "/search/germplasm"). body Named list. search request body. poll_interval Numeric. Seconds polling attempts. Default 2. max_polls Integer. Maximum polling attempts giving . Default 30.

title: Internal: POST Search to a BrAPI Endpoint — brapi_post_search
text: tibble search results.

title: brapiR2 (development version)
text: Initial release full BrAPI v2.1 endpoint coverage. Core module: brapi_programs(), brapi_trials(), brapi_studies(), brapi_locations(), brapi_seasons(), brapi_lists(), brapi_people(), brapi_server_info(). Germplasm module: brapi_germplasm(), brapi_germplasm_pedigree(), brapi_germplasm_progeny(), brapi_crosses(), brapi_crossing_projects(), brapi_seed_lots(), brapi_search_germplasm(). Phenotyping module: brapi_observation_units(), brapi_observations(), brapi_observation_variables(), brapi_traits(), brapi_scales(), brapi_methods(), brapi_images(), brapi_events(), brapi_search_observations(), brapi_search_variables(). Genotyping module: brapi_samples(), brapi_variants(), brapi_variant_sets(), brapi_calls(), brapi_call_sets(), brapi_references(), brapi_reference_sets(), brapi_allele_matrix(), brapi_search_variants(), brapi_search_calls(). Convenience functions: brapi_study_data() (wide-format phenotype table), brapi_get_dosage_matrix(), brapi_get_marker_map(). Stateless brapi_connection() — global state side effects. Automatic pagination GET endpoints. Async search handling (202 status + polling). Built-response caching brapi_cache_enable(). Parallel batch fetching brapi_fetch_parallel().
